sutta: monks there are these 8 assemblies 1 8 assemblies of nobles or brahmins of householders of recluses of devas of the 4 royal devas of the 33 devas of maharas and of brahmas now monks i call to mind having visited many hundreds of times an assembly of nobles of brahmins of householders of recluses of the 4 royal devas of deva supper amaras of brahmas and before even i had seated myself among them or had spoken to them or had engaged them in conversation whatever their color that i became whatever their language that became mine and i instructed them incited them to rouse them and gladden them with dhamma discourse and they knew me not when i spoke but reasoned among themselves saying who is this who speaks man or deva then when i instructed incited raos and gladdened them with dhamma discourse i vanished and they knew me not when i was gone but questioned each other who is this who has vanished man or deva monks these are the 8 assemblies that's the end of the sutta
commentary: this is another interesting suta where the buddha shows that he uses psychic power to visit various types of devas and men and he assumed their form and he spoke their language and he gave them dharma teachings and then after that he just vanished and they were surprised who when they were they're wondering who he was and he just disappeared the next utah is
